usecases.name: Milestone & critical-path tracking agent
usecases.trigger: scheduled (nightly) + event (any predecessor task status/date change via Smartsheet webhook)
usecases.what_ai_does: Pulls the dependency-enabled Smartsheet plan, recomputes critical path and float, and flags tasks now on/near the critical path or at risk of slipping; drafts a plain-language 'what changed and why it matters' note. The recompute math and dependency logic come from Smartsheet's engine (validated path); the LLM explains, prioritizes, and proposes — it does not move the baseline.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified plan/task names) for the narrative; Smartsheet API for the actual critical-path computation
usecases.integration: Smartsheet API (sheets, cells, dependencies, webhooks), CTMS, RAG (project charter / timeline plan-of-record)
usecases.human: PM reviews flagged critical-path changes; baseline/milestone-date edits require PM approval before the agent writes back
usecases.value: Early warning on schedule risk; no more manual nightly Gantt re-reads; the critical path is always current and explained

usecases.name: Study-event timeline auto-update & notification (data cut, DB lock, milestone slip)
usecases.trigger: event (data-cut declared, soft/hard DB lock, milestone marked complete or slipped) caught by webhook or system event
usecases.what_ai_does: A gateway agent recomputes the downstream plan in Smartsheet, drafts targeted notifications (who is now blocked, what shifts), and posts them to the right channels. The Smartsheet API performs the dependency recompute; the LLM composes the human-readable alert and routes it by role. Date commits to the plan-of-record are proposed, not auto-applied to contractual milestones.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (Local if event payload carries unblinded/subject context; Cloud for de-identified scheduling text); Smartsheet API for recompute
usecases.integration: Smartsheet API (webhooks + cell updates + update requests), Outlook/Exchange, Teams/Slack, EDC/CTMS event hooks
usecases.human: Lead approves the recomputed downstream dates and the notification list before contractual/regulated milestone dates are committed
usecases.value: Hours-to-minutes turnaround on cascade updates after a data cut or lock; stakeholders notified consistently, nobody learns of a slip late

usecases.name: Status-report generation (study & program)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (weekly/biweekly study status; monthly program report)
usecases.what_ai_does: Aggregates milestone status, enrollment, open risks/issues, action-item aging, and vendor deliverable status into a drafted status report in the house template, citing the plan-of-record from RAG. Drafts only — numbers are pulled from systems of record, not invented; flagged for the PM to confirm.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified status metrics and narrative); Local if the report embeds unblinded interim figures
usecases.integration: Smartsheet API, CTMS, RAG (templates + charter), SharePoint, Outlook
usecases.human: PM edits and signs off before distribution; any RED metric (e.g., reported enrollment of record) is verified against the system of record
usecases.value: Eliminates the weekly manual status-deck assembly; consistent, on-template reports across all studies in minutes

usecases.name: Action-item & decision extraction from email/meetings
usecases.trigger: event (new meeting transcript posted; flagged email thread) or on-demand
usecases.what_ai_does: Reads meeting transcripts/notes and email threads, extracts action items (owner, due date), and logged decisions; classifies and de-duplicates against the existing action log, then proposes new rows. Drafts/classifies only; it does not assign accountability or set committed dates without owner confirmation.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (emails/transcripts may contain PHI, site, or unblinded references — keep on-prem)
usecases.integration: Outlook/Exchange, Teams transcripts, Smartsheet (action/decision log), SharePoint
usecases.human: Owner/PM confirms each extracted action and decision before it is logged as the record
usecases.value: Nothing falls through the cracks post-meeting; decision provenance captured automatically; far less manual minute-mining

usecases.name: Meeting agenda & minutes drafting
usecases.trigger: scheduled (before recurring governance/team meetings) + event (after the meeting for minutes)
usecases.what_ai_does: Builds the agenda from open action items, risks due for review, and prior-meeting carryover (pulled from RAG/Smartsheet); after the meeting, drafts minutes from the transcript with decisions and new actions cross-linked. Draft artifacts only.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (transcripts/attendee context on-prem); Cloud for de-identified agenda skeletons if preferred
usecases.integration: Outlook/Exchange (calendar), Teams transcripts, Smartsheet (actions/risks), SharePoint, RAG
usecases.human: Chair reviews/edits agenda and approves minutes before circulation
usecases.value: Standardized agendas and same-day minutes with zero clerical overhead; tight action-item continuity meeting to meeting

usecases.name: Vendor/CRO oversight & deliverable tracking
usecases.trigger: scheduled (weekly deliverable sweep) + event (deliverable received / due-date approaching)
usecases.what_ai_does: Tracks contracted deliverables and SLAs against SOW/contract obligations stored in RAG, flags upcoming/overdue/at-risk items, and drafts oversight-log entries and follow-up emails to vendors. Classifies status and drafts chasers; humans own escalation and acceptance decisions.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified deliverable/SLA text) grounded by RAG over SOWs/contracts
usecases.integration: RAG (SOW/contract/oversight plan), Smartsheet (deliverable tracker), Outlook, SharePoint
usecases.human: Oversight lead confirms deliverable acceptance and approves any escalation or SLA-breach notice
usecases.value: Continuous, documented vendor oversight aligned to ICH E6(R3) sponsor-oversight expectations; no missed deliverables or unlogged follow-ups

usecases.name: Resourcing & capacity conflict detection
usecases.trigger: scheduled (weekly) + event (plan change that shifts effort dates)
usecases.what_ai_does: Reads allocations across studies (programmers, PK scientists, clinic capacity) from the resourcing sheets, detects over-allocation and contention windows against recomputed timelines, and drafts a leveling recommendation. Proposes options; the LLM does not reassign people.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified roles/effort) + Smartsheet API for the date math
usecases.integration: Smartsheet API (resource/allocation sheets, dependencies), CTMS, HR/PSA resource system if present
usecases.human: Resource manager approves any reassignment or re-leveling before it becomes the plan
usecases.value: Surfaces double-booking before it bites; protects shared early-phase resources (PK lab, beds, programmers) across parallel cohorts

usecases.name: Risk register upkeep & RBQM linkage
usecases.trigger: scheduled (monthly review prep) + event (KRI/QTL breach signal, new issue, milestone slip)
usecases.what_ai_does: Keeps the risk register current: drafts new risk entries from issues/slips and from RBQM KRI/QTL breach signals, proposes likelihood/impact and updated status/mitigations, and prepares the review packet. Drafts and classifies; risk acceptance and scoring sign-off stay human per ICH E6(R3).
usecases.engine: Local frozen model if breach signals carry unblinded/site detail; Cloud for de-identified risk narrative
usecases.integration: Smartsheet (risk register), RBQM/KRI dashboard, RAG (risk-assessment plan, integrated quality risk mgmt), Outlook
usecases.human: Quality/PM lead approves risk scoring, mitigations, and closures; QTL-breach actions go through the quality decision path
usecases.value: A living, defensible risk register tied to live RBQM signals — inspection-ready and not a stale spreadsheet

usecases.name: Budget & PO / accrual tracking
usecases.trigger: scheduled (monthly close prep) + event (invoice received, PO threshold approached, milestone-based payment triggered)
usecases.what_ai_does: Reconciles vendor invoices and POs against budget lines and milestone-based payment schedules from RAG/contracts, drafts accrual estimates and budget-vs-actual commentary, and flags overruns or payments due on completed milestones. Drafts and flags only; reported financial actuals come from the finance/ERP system of record.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified budget lines/commentary) grounded by RAG over contracts/payment schedules
usecases.integration: Finance/ERP system of record, Smartsheet (budget tracker), RAG (contract/payment schedule), Outlook
usecases.human: Finance/PM verifies every reported actual and approves accruals/payment triggers in the system of record — LLM is out of the commit path
usecases.value: Faster, better-supported monthly accruals and early overrun warnings, with all reported numbers staying tool-of-record authoritative

usecases.name: Cross-study portfolio rollup
usecases.trigger: scheduled (weekly/monthly portfolio cadence) + on-demand (leadership ask)
usecases.what_ai_does: Aggregates milestones, slip status, risk heat, enrollment, and budget health across all active studies into a portfolio dashboard narrative and exception list (which studies are red and why). Summarizes and ranks; underlying metrics come from each study's system of record.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified, rolled-up metrics) + Smartsheet API for cross-sheet aggregation
usecases.integration: Smartsheet API (cross-sheet reports/dashboards), CTMS, finance system, RAG
usecases.human: Portfolio lead reviews the rollup; any escalated exception is confirmed before leadership distribution
usecases.value: One coherent portfolio view across many parallel early-phase studies without manual cross-sheet stitching; exceptions surface first

usecases.name: Action-item SLA chasing & escalation
usecases.trigger: scheduled (daily) — overdue/approaching-due action items
usecases.what_ai_does: Scans the action/decision log for items nearing or past due, drafts polite reminder emails to owners and an escalation summary for items breaching SLA. Drafts and routes; escalation decisions remain human.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified action titles/owners); Local if action text references PHI/unblinded context
usecases.integration: Smartsheet (action log), Outlook/Exchange, Teams
usecases.human: PM approves escalations; owners receive AI-drafted reminders (low-risk auto-send optional per policy)
usecases.value: Closes the loop on stalled actions automatically; PMs stop being the manual nag for routine reminders

usecases.name: Milestone-slip root-cause & schedule-recovery proposal
usecases.trigger: event (milestone marked slipped or critical-path date moves beyond tolerance)
usecases.what_ai_does: On a confirmed slip, correlates likely drivers from linked actions, risks, vendor deliverables, and recent status notes, and drafts candidate recovery options (fast-track, re-sequence, resource add) with their timeline impact computed by Smartsheet. Diagnoses and proposes scenarios; it does not commit a recovery plan.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (Local for any unblinded/site root-cause detail; Cloud for scenario narrative) + Smartsheet API for impact modeling
usecases.integration: Smartsheet API (what-if date impact, dependencies), Risk/action logs, RAG (timeline plan), Outlook
usecases.human: PM/lead selects and approves the recovery scenario before it becomes the revised plan-of-record
usecases.value: Turns a slip alert into actionable, pre-modeled recovery choices in minutes, shortening the decide-and-replan cycle

usecases.name: Auto-classify & file to the DIA TMF Reference Model
usecases.trigger: event — new document lands in an intake folder / SharePoint dropzone / scan inbox / email attachment
usecases.what_ai_does: OCRs and reads the document, then classifies it to the correct TMF Reference Model v3.2.0 Zone > Section > artifact ID and extracts filing metadata (study, site, country, document date, version). Proposes the filing as a draft with a confidence score; the validated eTMF and a human declare the record-of-truth. High-confidence routine artifacts can be batch pre-staged for one-click human approval.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (documents routinely contain PHI/PII; classify on-prem) — only fully de-identified, hard-to-classify edge cases optionally escalate to Cloud Claude
usecases.integration: SharePoint/scan-inbox -> RAG/classifier -> eTMF (Veeva Vault eTMF / Montrium) write to staging + approval queue; Outlook for attachment intake
usecases.human: Document controller verifies classification + metadata and approves the final filing into the record-of-truth (RED on the filing action itself); low-confidence items always go to a person
usecases.value: Eliminates the largest manual eTMF burden (sorting/indexing/metadata entry), cuts mis-filing, and keeps the TMF contemporaneous instead of backlogged

usecases.name: TMF completeness / QC & missing-document detection
usecases.trigger: scheduled (nightly/weekly) + event on milestone change (site activation, FPI, data cut)
usecases.what_ai_does: Compares filed artifacts against the expected-document index (TMF plan / artifact-to-milestone map) for the current study phase and each site, computes per-zone completeness %, lists missing/overdue/placeholder artifacts, and flags quality defects (wrong version, unsigned, illegible scan, wrong date). Drafts the gap list and a QC summary; does not change any record.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (operates over filed PHI-bearing documents and metadata) + Validated tool for the authoritative status fields
usecases.integration: eTMF API (artifact/metadata read), Smartsheet/TMF-plan as expected-document index, dashboard
usecases.human: TMF lead reviews the gap report and assigns remediation owners; any status correction is made by a human in the validated system
usecases.value: Continuous (not pre-inspection) completeness, fewer late filings, and an always-current view of TMF health required by ICH E6(R3)

usecases.name: Expiring-document alerts (CV / training / license / certs)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (daily) sweep of expiry-dated artifacts
usecases.what_ai_does: Extracts expiry dates from CVs, GCP-training certificates, medical licenses, lab certifications/normal-range docs, and IATA shipping certs; tracks them against thresholds (e.g. 90/60/30 days) and drafts targeted reminder notices to the responsible site/role. Maintains a credential-currency register. Sends alerts only; renewals and re-filing stay manual.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (CVs/licenses contain personal data)
usecases.integration: eTMF API, Outlook/Teams for notifications, register in Smartsheet
usecases.tier: GREEN (alerting) / AMBER (the extracted expiry metadata is human-verified before it drives a register)
usecases.human: CRA/site manager confirms the flagged expiries and chases renewals; nobody's record is changed automatically
usecases.value: Prevents lapsed-credential inspection findings — a common, avoidable Major finding — with zero manual diary-keeping

usecases.name: SOP / WI / study-document ingestion into the RAG knowledge base
usecases.trigger: event — a controlled document is approved/effective or superseded in the DMS
usecases.what_ai_does: Parses the approved document, chunks and embeds it into the permissioned local vector store with metadata (doc ID, version, effective date, owner, applicable studies), and links/citations back to the source. On supersession, retires the old version's chunks so retrieval never serves obsolete guidance. Indexing only — it authors nothing.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model + local embeddings (knowledge base is on-prem and permissioned; SOPs are internal IP)
usecases.integration: DMS/SharePoint controlled-docs library -> local RAG vector store; effective-version feed from the DMS
usecases.human: QA owns which version is 'effective'; ingestion follows the DMS state of truth, so no separate approval is needed beyond normal doc-control
usecases.value: Turns the SOP/WI library into an instantly queryable, always-current-version knowledge base and is the foundation for compliance Q&A

usecases.name: Compliance Q&A over SOPs/WIs ("what does SOP-12 require for X?")
usecases.trigger: on-demand — staff question via chat/portal
usecases.what_ai_does: Retrieves the relevant SOP/WI chunks and answers with the requirement, quoting and citing the exact document ID, version, and section; flags when multiple/conflicting documents apply and when a referenced doc is superseded. Answers are guidance/navigation, explicitly 'verify against the controlled document' — it never overrides the SOP.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model over local RAG (default; keeps internal IP on-prem). Cloud Claude only for de-identified, non-proprietary general-GCP reasoning if ever needed.
usecases.integration: Local RAG store, chat/portal UI, link-out to the DMS source
usecases.human: User judgment; for regulatory-binding interpretation the answer routes the user to QA/RA
usecases.value: Cuts time-to-answer for 'how do we do X per our SOP' from hours of searching to seconds, with citations — improves consistency and onboarding

usecases.name: Version / superseded-document control & reference reconciliation
usecases.trigger: event — new version of an SOP / protocol amendment / ICF / plan is filed
usecases.what_ai_does: Detects the supersession, diffs old vs new to summarize what changed, identifies downstream documents and TMF references that cite the prior version, and drafts a reconciliation checklist (which docs need re-pointing, which trainings re-trigger). Proposes marking prior versions superseded; the human and validated system execute the status change.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (internal controlled docs) + Cloud Claude optionally for a de-identified plain-language change summary
usecases.integration: DMS/eTMF API, training system, Smartsheet checklist
usecases.human: Doc-control/QA approves the supersession status change and confirms the reconciliation actions in the validated system (RED on the actual status write)
usecases.value: Stops obsolete-version use and dangling references — a frequent root cause of TMF and protocol-compliance findings

usecases.name: Inspection-readiness gap analysis
usecases.trigger: scheduled (monthly) + on-demand ahead of an audit/inspection
usecases.what_ai_does: Runs a deeper readiness scan: completeness by zone, overdue/placeholder artifacts, unsigned or out-of-window documents, expiring credentials, superseded-version references, and known inspector hot-spots; produces a prioritized remediation plan with owners and a narrative readiness summary. Assessment and drafting only; remediation is human-executed.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (operates over the live TMF) + Cloud Claude for a de-identified executive readiness narrative
usecases.integration: eTMF API, Smartsheet remediation tracker, dashboard, Outlook for the summary
usecases.human: QA / clinical-ops lead reviews the gap analysis, validates findings, and owns the remediation plan and timeline
usecases.value: Shifts inspection prep from a frantic manual TMF audit to a continuous, scored, prioritized readiness posture

usecases.name: Redaction / PII-PHI detection before any cloud use
usecases.trigger: event — any document or text about to be sent to the Cloud Claude backend (a guardrail in the gateway path)
usecases.what_ai_does: Scans content with Presidio (incl. MedicalNER and DICOM/image redaction) to detect PII/PHI; if found, blocks the cloud route and keeps processing local, or produces a de-identified version for cloud reasoning with a logged mapping. Acts as the enforcement point of the route-by-sensitivity rule; never the sole gate for declaring data 'safe'.
usecases.engine: Validated/deterministic tool (Presidio) at the gateway, augmented by the Local frozen model for context-aware checks; this is what GATES Cloud Claude
usecases.integration: LiteLLM-class gateway pre-call hook, Presidio service, audit log
usecases.tier: AMBER (a human/QA spot-checks de-identification quality before any de-identified corpus is routinely cloud-processed; PHI never reaches cloud regardless)
usecases.human: Privacy/QA validates de-identification adequacy on samples; flagged-as-PHI items are hard-routed local with no human override needed
usecases.value: Hard technical guarantee that PHI/PII never egresses to the cloud — the linchpin that makes the hybrid HIPAA-defensible

usecases.name: Document QC linter (signatures, dates, legibility, completeness fields)
usecases.trigger: event — at point of filing / intake, before a doc reaches the approval queue
usecases.what_ai_does: Checks each incoming document for required signatures and dates, signature-vs-event date windows, page completeness, legibility/scan quality, correct form version, and required fields populated; drafts a QC finding note for any defect and routes it back to the originator. Flags and explains only; it does not pass/fail the official record.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (PHI-bearing source-adjacent docs)
usecases.integration: Intake pipeline, eTMF staging, Outlook/Teams for return-to-originator
usecases.human: Document controller confirms the QC finding and decides accept/reject; final QC status is set in the validated eTMF
usecases.value: Catches the everyday quality defects (missing signature/date, wrong version, bad scan) at the door instead of at inspection

usecases.name: Email & attachment auto-routing to the eTMF intake
usecases.trigger: event — inbound email to a study mailbox with a fileable attachment
usecases.what_ai_does: Triages study mailboxes, recognizes fileable artifacts (e.g. a returned signed form, a site cert, a vendor qualification doc), extracts study/site context, and routes the attachment into the classification/QC pipeline with proposed metadata. Triage and routing only; filing remains the human-approved step downstream.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (email bodies/attachments may contain PHI)
usecases.integration: Outlook/Exchange, intake pipeline -> classifier -> eTMF staging
usecases.human: Owner of the intake queue reviews routed items; filing approval happens at the classify-and-file step
usecases.value: Closes the 'documents stuck in inboxes' gap, the most common reason artifacts arrive in the TMF late or not at all

usecases.name: Translation / certified-copy & localization control
usecases.trigger: event — a non-English or country-specific document is filed (e.g. local ICF, regulatory approval, ethics correspondence)
usecases.what_ai_does: Detects language and document type, drafts a working translation/summary for reviewer context, checks that a certified translation and the corresponding source/certified-copy artifact are both present and correctly cross-linked, and flags missing pairs. The certified translation of record stays a validated, human/vendor deliverable — the LLM never produces the official translation.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (regulatory/ethics docs, possibly PII) — working summary; Cloud Claude only on de-identified text
usecases.integration: eTMF API, translation-vendor tracker in Smartsheet
usecases.human: Regulatory/QA confirms the certified translation and certified-copy pairing in the record-of-truth
usecases.value: Prevents the common multi-country finding of missing certified translations or unlinked source/translation pairs

usecases.name: TMF audit-trail & filing-activity summarizer
usecases.trigger: scheduled (weekly) + on-demand for a specific period/site
usecases.what_ai_does: Reads the eTMF/Part-11 audit trail and summarizes filing activity, backlog age, who-filed-what, recurring rejection reasons, and bottlenecks; drafts a plain-language TMF-health narrative and trend for governance. Reads and summarizes the validated audit trail — it never edits it, and reported metrics tie back to the system of record.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (audit data references subjects/sites/people) + Cloud Claude for a de-identified executive narrative
usecases.integration: eTMF/Part-11 audit-trail API, Smartsheet/dashboard, Outlook for the digest
usecases.tier: GREEN (read-only summary; the audit trail itself is the immutable validated record)
usecases.human: TMF lead reviews the narrative before it goes to governance
usecases.value: Turns an opaque audit trail into an actionable weekly TMF-health and bottleneck signal without manual log-trawling

usecases.name: Scheduled CDISC conformance run + AI finding-triage (SDTM/ADaM/Define-XML)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (nightly + on every new SDTM/ADaM build or data refresh)
usecases.what_ai_does: Pinnacle 21 (validated) produces the authoritative findings file (Error/Warning/Notice) against SDTMIG v3.4 / ADaMIG v1.3 / Define-XML v2.1 / FDA + PMDA rules. The LOCAL LLM then clusters findings by rule and domain, diffs against the last clean run to surface only NEW findings, drafts a plain-language explanation and a proposed disposition (real defect vs expected/explained-in-cSDRG/ADRG) grounded in the reviewer guides via RAG, and routes each cluster to the right programmer. It never changes the pass/fail verdict.
usecases.engine: Validated tool (Pinnacle 21) for pass/fail + Local frozen model for triage/draft
usecases.integration: Pinnacle 21 Enterprise output share, SDTM/ADaM datasets, Define-XML, cSDRG/ADRG in RAG store, programmer worklist/Smartsheet, Part 11 audit trail
usecases.human: Stat programmer / CDISC lead reviews and accepts/edits every triage disposition; the conformance report itself is the validated record-of-truth (RED) and is never altered by AI
usecases.value: Hundreds of repeat/expected findings get pre-explained and the genuinely new ones surface instantly each build — turning a multi-hour manual triage into minutes, with a fully audited disposition log ready for the cSDRG/ADRG.

usecases.name: Cross-form / edit-check / logical-consistency surveillance
usecases.trigger: event (each EDC data load / DTA transfer) + scheduled daily
usecases.what_ai_does: Deterministic edit-check and cross-form logic (the validated check battery / SQL) runs first and flags hard inconsistencies (e.g., AE start before first dose, lab date outside visit window, EX dose vs randomized arm, pregnancy-test logic, visit-sequence breaks). The LOCAL LLM then reads the flagged subject-level rows, explains the likely cause in clinical language, distinguishes a true data error from an explainable protocol event, drafts a candidate query text, and proposes routing to DM or the site. Cross-form *logic* design can be drafted by Cloud Claude from de-identified specs.
usecases.engine: Validated check logic + Local frozen model (subject-level rows stay local); Cloud Claude only for de-identified check-design
usecases.integration: EDC/CTMS, Data Review Plan + edit-check spec in RAG, query manager, DM worklist, audit trail
usecases.human: DM / data reviewer approves the query before it is issued; no value is changed by AI
usecases.value: Catches logical impossibilities and cross-domain contradictions continuously instead of at lock, with draft queries ready for one-click human approval.

usecases.name: PK timing (actual vs nominal) and BLQ/LLOQ rule checking
usecases.trigger: event (each bioanalytical / PK concentration load) + scheduled
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated logic computes actual-minus-nominal sampling deviations and applies the SAP/PK-plan BLQ/LLOQ handling rules (e.g., pre-first-dose BLQ=0, embedded BLQ vs trailing BLQ, LLOQ substitution) deterministically. The LOCAL LLM triages timing deviations beyond tolerance, explains each against the PK sampling schedule and t1/2 expectations via RAG, flags mis-applied or ambiguous BLQ flags for the PK scientist, and drafts the discrepancy note — it does not set the analysis value (that re-derives from the validated rule / Phoenix downstream).
usecases.engine: Validated rule logic + Phoenix WinNonlin downstream (engine of record) + Local frozen model for triage
usecases.integration: ADPC/PC datasets, PK Analysis Plan + BLQ/LLOQ rules in RAG, bioanalytical transfer, PK scientist worklist, audit trail
usecases.human: PK scientist reviews timing deviations and confirms BLQ handling; reported PK values come from the validated engine, never the LLM
usecases.value: Timing-deviation and BLQ edge cases (the classic source of NCA errors) are surfaced and explained every load, protecting the integrity of the concentration dataset feeding Phoenix.

usecases.name: Lab / IXRS(IRT) / PK-sample reconciliation
usecases.trigger: event (each lab, IXRS, or bioanalytical transfer) + scheduled weekly
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated reconciliation logic performs 100% record-count, key, and value matching across sources (central-lab transfer vs LB, IXRS dispensing/kit vs EX/randomization, PK samples drawn per IXRS/lab manifest vs concentrations received/PC). The LOCAL LLM triages the unreconciled set, classifies each mismatch (missing sample, unit mismatch, duplicate, late transfer, mis-keyed accession), explains the likely root cause, and drafts the reconciliation finding + routing to the lab, DM, or PK. It does not adjudicate the matched record-of-truth.
usecases.engine: Validated reconciliation logic + Local frozen model for mismatch triage
usecases.integration: Central-lab transfer, IXRS/IRT, bioanalytical manifest, LB/EX/PC datasets, reconciliation spec in RAG, lab + DM worklists, audit trail
usecases.human: DM / PK scientist confirms each reconciliation resolution; the matched dataset is the validated record
usecases.value: Sample-accountability and kit/dispensing gaps are caught and root-caused continuously, preventing missing-data surprises at lock and supporting full PK sample accountability.

usecases.name: Query-management oversight, aging & SLA escalation
usecases.trigger: scheduled daily + threshold breach
usecases.what_ai_does: Pulls open-query metadata from the query manager (de-identified: counts, ages, status, site, query type) and lets the LLM compute aging buckets, detect SLA breaches, summarize the open-query landscape, predict which queries threaten the next data-review or lock milestone, draft escalation emails, and prioritize the DM worklist. Subject-level query text stays LOCAL; aggregate metadata may go CLOUD for sharper trend reasoning. It does not open, answer, or close queries.
usecases.engine: Local (query text) / Cloud Claude (de-identified aggregate metadata)
usecases.integration: Query manager/EDC, CTMS milestones, Outlook/Teams, Smartsheet/DM dashboard, audit trail
usecases.human: DM lead reviews the prioritized list and approves escalations; query state changes stay human
usecases.value: Aging queries stop slipping past lock-critical thresholds; DM gets a daily, ranked, milestone-aware worklist and ready-to-send escalations.

usecases.name: Duplicate / outlier / range / digit-preference checks
usecases.trigger: scheduled (each refresh) + event on load
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated statistical/rule logic computes range violations, within-subject and population outliers (e.g., robust z / IQR), duplicate records, and digit-preference / rounding patterns. The LOCAL LLM triages each flag on subject-level data: separates a plausible-but-extreme physiological value from a likely transcription error, explains it against the variable's expected range and the subject's own trajectory (RAG), and drafts a query or a 'no-action, explained' note. Method design and thresholds can be drafted via Cloud Claude on de-identified specs.
usecases.engine: Validated statistical logic + Local frozen model for triage
usecases.integration: SDTM/ADaM + raw EDC, range/edit specs in RAG, DM/central-monitoring dashboard, audit trail
usecases.human: Data reviewer / central monitor confirms each outlier disposition; no value altered by AI
usecases.value: Outliers and duplicates are explained in context rather than dumped as raw flags, and digit-preference checks feed the central-monitoring (fraud/quality) signal — every refresh.

usecases.name: Data-quality KRI / QTL trend dashboard upkeep (RBQM)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (daily/weekly) + threshold breach
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated metric definitions compute the data-quality KRIs (site-level: query rate, query age, missing-data %, overdue forms, protocol-deviation rate, time-to-entry) and QTLs (trial-level acceptable ranges per ICH E6(R3)). The LLM narrates the dashboard: explains which KRI/QTL moved and why, distinguishes signal from noise, drafts the RBQM/central-monitoring narrative, and proposes a follow-up action. Aggregate site-level metrics may go CLOUD; any drill-down to subject rows stays LOCAL. Thresholds and breach status come from the validated metric layer.
usecases.engine: Validated metric layer + Cloud Claude (aggregate narrative) / Local (subject drill-down)
usecases.integration: EDC/CTMS, RBQM/CSM platform, KRI/QTL spec in RAG, Smartsheet/dashboard, audit trail
usecases.human: Central monitor / RBQM lead reviews narrative and decides actions; QTL/KRI breach status is from the validated tool
usecases.value: Turns a metric wall into a weekly plain-language RBQM narrative with proposed actions — the 'demonstrate, don't just describe' centralized monitoring E6(R3) now expects.

usecases.name: Statistical / central-monitoring anomaly signals (site outliers, enrollment & visit anomalies)
usecases.trigger: scheduled weekly
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated central statistical monitoring (CSM) computes site-vs-site outlier statistics, unusual variance/means, enrollment and visit-pattern anomalies, and inlier/over-uniformity signals. The LOCAL LLM triages the ranked signals, explains each candidate (operational cause vs data-integrity concern), correlates across KRIs, and drafts a site-level follow-up or a monitoring-trigger memo. Subject/site-level data stays LOCAL; only de-identified statistical summaries may reach CLOUD.
usecases.engine: Validated CSM engine + Local frozen model for signal triage
usecases.integration: RBQM/CSM platform, EDC/CTMS, site-monitoring worklist, KRI history in RAG, audit trail
usecases.human: Central monitor / lead reviews each signal and decides on a site action; the statistical signal is from the validated engine
usecases.value: Surfaces data-integrity and operational anomalies a tabular KRI report would bury, with a drafted, explainable rationale for any monitoring trigger.

usecases.name: Pre-lock data-review listing generation, diff & sign-off packet
usecases.trigger: scheduled in the pre-lock window + on-demand before each blinded data review
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated programs produce the SAP/Data-Review-Plan-defined listings (blinded). The LOCAL LLM diffs the current listings against the prior review run, summarizes what changed and what remains open, maps each open item to its query/disposition, drafts the blinded-data-review meeting package and the lock-readiness checklist, and flags any SAP-required check not yet evidenced. It does not generate or alter the listing values.
usecases.engine: Validated listing programs + Local frozen model for diff/summary/packet
usecases.integration: ADaM/SDTM, Data Review Plan + SAP in RAG, blinded-review minutes, lock checklist, audit trail
usecases.human: Lead biostatistician chairs the blinded review and signs the lock-readiness decision; blinded decisions are recorded with timestamp before unblinding
usecases.value: Each blinded data review starts from a what-changed-since-last-time summary and an evidence-mapped lock checklist, compressing pre-lock cleaning and de-risking the lock decision.

usecases.name: Discrepancy triage, classification & routing to DM
usecases.trigger: event (any new flag from any check above) + scheduled sweep
usecases.what_ai_does: Acts as the unifying triage layer across all validated checks (conformance, edit-check, reconciliation, outlier, timing). The LOCAL LLM de-duplicates overlapping flags, classifies each discrepancy by type, severity, and likely owner, drafts the query/finding text grounded in the relevant SOP/spec via RAG, proposes a route (DM, site, PK, programming, medical coding), and assembles a single ranked DM worklist. It routes and drafts; it does not resolve or close.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model (subject-level) — orchestrating validated-tool outputs
usecases.integration: All check outputs, query manager, DM/programming worklists, SOPs/specs in RAG, Outlook/Teams, audit trail
usecases.human: DM / data reviewer approves classification and routing; resolution and closure stay human in the validated system
usecases.value: Replaces fragmented per-tool flag lists with one de-duplicated, classified, pre-routed, draft-query worklist — the single biggest day-to-day time sink in DM oversight.

usecases.name: MedDRA / WHODrug auto-coding QC & uncoded-term triage
usecases.trigger: event (each AE/CM/MH load) + scheduled
usecases.what_ai_does: The coding system/dictionary (validated, versioned MedDRA/WHODrug) remains the source of coded values. The LOCAL LLM reviews the QC output: flags suspicious auto-codes, surfaces uncoded/queried verbatim terms, suggests candidate codes WITH the dictionary path for a human coder to confirm, and drafts the coding query. Verbatim AE/medication terms are subject-level and stay LOCAL. It proposes; it never assigns the code of record.
usecases.engine: Validated coding dictionary/tool + Local frozen model for QC triage/suggestion
usecases.integration: Safety/coding database, MedDRA/WHODrug (versioned), AE/CM/MH SDTM, coder worklist, audit trail
usecases.human: Medical coder confirms/assigns every code; the validated coding tool holds the record-of-truth
usecases.value: Speeds the uncoded-term backlog and catches questionable auto-codes early, while the coded value of record stays under the validated dictionary and a human coder.

usecases.name: SDTM<->Phoenix<->ADaM PK numeric cross-traceability reconciliation
usecases.trigger: event (each PK parameter / ADPP build) + pre-lock
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated reconciliation checks confirm PK parameters match across PP <-> Phoenix WinNonlin output (engine of record) <-> ADPP, and that RELREC PC<->PP linkage and time-after-dose derivations are consistent. The LOCAL LLM triages any mismatch, localizes where the chain broke (mapping, BLQ rule, rounding, parameter-code mapping), explains it, and drafts the finding to the PK scientist/programmer. The authoritative PK numbers always re-derive from Phoenix — the LLM never computes or 'fixes' a value.
usecases.engine: Phoenix WinNonlin (engine of record) + validated reconciliation logic + Local frozen model for mismatch triage
usecases.integration: PP/ADPP datasets, Phoenix output, RELREC, PK plan in RAG, PK/programming worklist, audit trail
usecases.human: PK scientist + lead biostatistician confirm the reconciliation; any reported PK number comes only from the validated engine, LLM out of the decision path
usecases.value: Guarantees the PK numbers that reach the CSR are identical and traceable across PP, Phoenix, and ADPP — the highest-stakes reconciliation in an early-phase clin-pharm study.

usecases.name: KRI/QTL definition & specification drafting
usecases.what_ai_does: Drafts KRI/QTL definitions, rationale, numerators/denominators, and starting thresholds from the protocol + risk assessment + SOP library (RAG); proposes the metric catalog and code stubs. Human risk-committee/biostat finalizes thresholds; the validated CSM platform computes them. LLM does not set thresholds of record.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified protocol text) + RAG
usecases.trigger: on-demand at study startup / protocol amendment
usecases.value: Cuts RBQM plan setup from weeks to days; consistent, traceable KRI/QTL library aligned to CtQ factors
usecases.integration: RAG (protocol/SOP/risk plan), CSM/RBQM platform, Smartsheet (risk register)
usecases.human: Risk committee + lead biostatistician approve thresholds and definitions

usecases.name: Threshold-breach signal triage & narrative
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated CSM/RBQM platform flags KRI/QTL breaches; LLM triages the queue, drafts the breach narrative (what breached, magnitude, trend, candidate causes from history), proposes severity/priority, and routes. The breach determination of record and any QTL excursion logged to the CSR stay human + validated tool.
usecases.engine: Local (subject-level/unblinded inputs) or Cloud (aggregate de-identified)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (e.g., weekly CSM refresh) + event (breach fired)
usecases.value: Faster, consistent triage; reviewers focus on real signals instead of dashboard scanning
usecases.integration: CSM/RBQM platform, EDC export, Smartsheet (action log), Outlook
usecases.human: Central monitor / medical monitor confirms breach and disposition

usecases.name: Central statistical monitoring signal interpretation (site outliers, digit preference, anomalies)
usecases.what_ai_does: Statistical engine computes site-outlier scores, digit-preference tests (volcano plots), variance/correlation anomalies, date implausibilities, and AE under-reporting. LLM narrates the ranked findings, clusters by site, and drafts a follow-up hypothesis list. The statistics themselves run in the validated/frozen engine, never the LLM.
usecases.engine: Validated stats tool computes; Local model narrates (subject-level data)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (data refresh)
usecases.value: Surfaces fraud/quality/training signals early; turns dense statistical output into actionable, plain-language leads
usecases.integration: CSM platform / SAS-R stats, EDC, RAG (site history)
usecases.human: Statistician + central monitor adjudicate each flagged site

usecases.name: Protocol-deviation surveillance & classification
usecases.what_ai_does: Classifies incoming deviations as major/minor and by category (eligibility, IP, visit-window, consent, procedure), maps to the protocol section, flags likely-important deviations per ICH E6(R3), and drafts the log entry. Human QA confirms major/important classifications; the deviation log of record is updated via validated system + human.
usecases.engine: Local (subject-identifiable deviation text) frozen model
usecases.trigger: event (deviation reported) / scheduled sweep of EDC + emails
usecases.value: Consistent classification, fewer missed important deviations, audit-ready rationale
usecases.integration: EDC/CTMS deviation module, RAG (protocol), Smartsheet/CTMS log
usecases.human: QA/clinical lead confirms major & 'important' deviation classification

usecases.name: Safety signal triage (AE/SAE volume, lab shifts)
usecases.what_ai_does: Triages aggregate AE/SAE volume trends and lab-shift patterns flagged by validated tools; drafts a triage note and prioritization for medical review. All reported safety numbers come from the validated safety DB/EDC with medical review; LLM is out of the number-generating and causality path.
usecases.engine: Local model on unblinded/subject-level inputs; validated tool for numbers
usecases.trigger: scheduled + event (SAE received / threshold hit)
usecases.value: Earlier focus on emerging safety patterns without exposing unblinded data to cloud
usecases.integration: Safety DB/EDC, lab vendor feed, RAG (protocol safety section)
usecases.human: Medical monitor / safety physician owns assessment and causality

usecases.name: Site performance & enrollment-metric reporting
usecases.what_ai_does: Summarizes site scorecards (enrollment vs plan, screen-fail, query aging, deviation rate, data-entry lag) from validated metrics into a narrative + recommended actions; drafts site-by-site commentary. Metrics computed by CTMS/EDC; LLM summarizes de-identified aggregates.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified aggregate metrics) + RAG
usecases.trigger: scheduled (weekly/monthly)
usecases.value: Standardized site oversight narratives; quick identification of lagging sites
usecases.integration: CTMS/EDC metrics, Smartsheet/Power BI, Outlook/SharePoint
usecases.human: Clinical operations lead reviews before distribution

usecases.name: Enrollment & visit-anomaly detection narrative
usecases.what_ai_does: Validated analytics detect enrollment spikes/clusters, visit-window violations, and implausible visit timing; LLM narrates the anomalies, links to candidate causes, and proposes monitoring follow-up. Detection math is in the validated tool; LLM only explains and routes.
usecases.engine: Local (subject-level timing data) + validated analytics
usecases.trigger: scheduled (data refresh)
usecases.value: Catches enrollment-pressure and visit-compliance issues early
usecases.integration: EDC/CTMS, CSM platform, Smartsheet (follow-up)
usecases.human: Central monitor reviews and assigns follow-up

usecases.name: CAPA drafting & tracking
usecases.what_ai_does: Drafts CAPA records (problem statement, root-cause options, proposed corrective/preventive actions, owners, due dates) from a signal/finding; tracks open CAPAs, flags overdue/aging items, drafts status updates. Effectiveness checks and CAPA closure of record are human-approved in the validated quality system.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified) + RAG (CAPA SOP)
usecases.trigger: event (finding raised) + scheduled aging sweep
usecases.value: Faster, better-structured CAPAs; nothing falls through the cracks on due dates
usecases.integration: Smartsheet/QMS (CAPA tracker, Smartsheet API), Outlook, RAG
usecases.human: QA owner approves root cause, actions, and closure

usecases.name: DSMB / safety-review packet assembly
usecases.what_ai_does: Assembles the DSMB/SRC packet shell: pulls validated tables/listings/figures, drafts the cover narrative, blinded vs unblinded section structure, and prior-action follow-up summary. All numbers/TLFs come from validated outputs; unblinded sections built on the LOCAL model only. LLM drafts narrative, never the numbers.
usecases.engine: Local model for unblinded sections; validated tools for all TLFs
usecases.trigger: scheduled (DSMB meeting cadence)
usecases.value: Cuts packet assembly time, consistent structure, reduces manual collation errors
usecases.integration: Stats/TLF outputs, safety DB, SharePoint, RAG (charter)
usecases.human: Unblinded statistician + medical writer + DSMB chair sign off

usecases.name: Monitoring-visit report (MVR) summarization
usecases.what_ai_does: Summarizes MVRs into structured findings (open issues, action items, deviations noted, site responsiveness) and a portfolio-level rollup of recurring themes across sites. De-identified MVR text; LLM produces drafts only, CRA/lead confirm findings of record.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified MVR text) + RAG
usecases.trigger: event (MVR filed) + scheduled rollup
usecases.value: Fast, consistent MVR digests; surfaces cross-site recurring issues
usecases.integration: CTMS/eTMF (MVR docs), SharePoint, Smartsheet (actions)
usecases.human: CRA / clinical lead verifies summarized findings

usecases.name: MVR follow-up & action-item tracking
usecases.what_ai_does: Extracts action items from MVRs, opens/updates tracker entries, links owners and due dates, drafts reminders for aging/overdue items, and flags items needing escalation. Writes go only to the non-regulated action tracker; eTMF record updates remain human + validated.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified) via MCP connectors
usecases.trigger: event (MVR filed) + scheduled aging sweep
usecases.value: Closes the loop on monitoring actions; reduces overdue follow-ups
usecases.integration: Smartsheet (Smartsheet API), Outlook, CTMS
usecases.human: CRA confirms action items and closures

usecases.name: QTL dashboard upkeep & RBQM oversight memo
usecases.what_ai_does: Keeps the QTL/KRI dashboard annotated: drafts change-log entries, narrates trend movements, flags definitions needing recalibration after amendments, and drafts the periodic RBQM oversight summary. Dashboard values come from the validated platform; LLM annotates de-identified aggregates only.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude (de-identified aggregates) + RAG
usecases.trigger: scheduled (dashboard refresh / monthly oversight)
usecases.value: Always-current, well-annotated oversight artifact; less manual dashboard maintenance
usecases.integration: CSM/RBQM platform, Power BI/Smartsheet, RAG (RBQM plan), SharePoint
usecases.human: RBQM/risk lead reviews oversight memo before filing

usecases.name: Model-agnostic gateway (LiteLLM-class) — single OpenAI-compatible front door
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on)
usecases.what_ai_does: Every agent/script calls ONE OpenAI-compatible endpoint; the gateway selects the backend per data-classification tag on the request, enforces per-key/per-model budgets and rate limits, retries/falls back, and emits a structured log line per call. It does not decide clinical content — it is the control plane that makes routing, cost, and audit non-bypassable.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (the gateway fronts both backends)
usecases.integration: Fronts LOCAL vLLM + CLOUD Claude API; emits to the audit/observability layer; virtual keys per agent/team
usecases.tier: GREEN (infrastructure; carries GREEN/AMBER/RED traffic but adds no autonomous authority)
usecases.human: Platform owner sets routing policy, budgets, and key scopes; changes to policy are reviewed/version-controlled
usecases.value: One choke point for routing, cost, fallback, and audit — swap or add a model without touching any agent; volatility hedge and the single place governance is enforced.

usecases.name: Data-classification router + Presidio PII/PHI guardrail (egress firewall)
usecases.trigger: event (on every inbound request, pre-call)
usecases.what_ai_does: Presidio scans payloads for identifiers and the policy engine routes by classification: PHI/unblinded/subject-level -> LOCAL only (deny-by-default to cloud); de-identified -> CLOUD-eligible. Misclassified-sensitive text is masked or blocked before egress. The guardrail classifies/redacts; it never interprets clinical meaning.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (decision is infra; payload then runs on the routed backend)
usecases.integration: Presidio recognizers (NHS/MRN/subject-ID custom entities), gateway policy store, audit trail
usecases.tier: AMBER (automated classify/redact; sensitive-data routing is safety-critical and is logged + sampled-reviewed)
usecases.human: DPO/privacy owner curates recognizers + allow/deny lists; periodic human audit of redaction false-negatives
usecases.value: Makes 'PHI/unblinded never leaves the building' a technical guarantee, not a policy promise — the core of the hybrid trust model.

usecases.name: LOCAL frozen open-weight backend (vLLM, air-gapped GPU)
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on)
usecases.what_ai_does: Serves on-prem inference for all sensitive/unblinded/subject-level drafting, classification, and triage with zero egress. Weights + decoding params + prompts are version-pinned so runs are reproducible for inspection. Generates drafts/triage only — never emits a reported number.
usecases.engine: Local frozen model
usecases.integration: vLLM OpenAI-compatible server on isolated GPU host; reachable only via the gateway; model-freeze registry
usecases.tier: GREEN/AMBER depending on task (RED data is allowed here because it never leaves; LLM still stays out of any reported-number decision)
usecases.human: Stat/IT owner approves each frozen model version into the registry before production use
usecases.value: Sovereignty + reproducibility: sensitive data stays on-prem and a frozen model lets you re-run an analysis-support task identically years later for an inspector.

usecases.name: CLOUD Claude backend (API under BAA, de-identified only)
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on)
usecases.what_ai_does: Handles hard reasoning, summarization, and code drafting on DE-IDENTIFIED text only (SOP Q&A, finding explanations, status-report prose). Reachable only after the classification router clears the payload. Drafts/reasons; humans/validated tools own any regulated output.
usecases.engine: Cloud Claude
usecases.integration: Anthropic Claude API with signed BAA; gateway-gated; prompt-caching enabled at the gateway
usecases.human: Reviewer accepts/rejects cloud drafts; BAA + DPIA owned by compliance; consumer/Max plans explicitly disallowed
usecases.value: Frontier reasoning for the non-sensitive 80% of text work without standing up frontier compute on-prem — ROI now, with PHI structurally excluded.

usecases.name: RAG knowledge base over SOPs/WIs/study docs (local, permissioned vector store)
usecases.trigger: event (re-index on document approval/supersede) + on-demand (queries)
usecases.what_ai_does: Ingests approved SOPs/WIs/protocols/plans, chunks + embeds them into a LOCAL vector store, and answers grounded compliance questions WITH CITATIONS to the source doc + version. Retrieval and synthesis only; it points to the controlled document — the controlled document, not the LLM, is authority.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (LOCAL embeddings/store; de-identified Q&A may use CLOUD; sensitive docs stay LOCAL)
usecases.integration: Local vector DB; SharePoint/eTMF as document source; permission-filtered by user role/study; version metadata from doc control
usecases.tier: GREEN (cited answers/summaries; never authoritative on its own)
usecases.human: QA owns the source-of-truth docs; users verify citations; no answer overrides the controlled document
usecases.value: Instant, cited 'what does SOP-X require?' across the whole library — cuts time-to-answer and onboarding, with permissions preventing cross-study/role leakage.

usecases.name: Orchestration layer (scheduled jobs + agent framework that fires the agents)
usecases.trigger: scheduled (cron) + event (webhooks: data cut, lock, document filed) + on-demand
usecases.what_ai_does: Runs the agents on schedule or on study events, chains gateway calls with connector tool-calls, enforces per-agent guardrails/timeouts, and drops anything that touches a record-of-truth into the approval queue instead of acting. Orchestrates; it does not grant agents write authority beyond their tier.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (orchestrator is code; calls models via gateway)
usecases.integration: cron + Python / n8n / Power Automate; webhooks from EDC/CTMS/eTMF; queue + audit hooks; MCP tool registry
usecases.tier: AMBER (automation harness; individual jobs carry their own tier; RED actions are intercepted to the queue)
usecases.human: Ops owner enables/schedules each agent; RED-tier outputs always land in the approval queue, never auto-applied
usecases.value: Turns one-off prompts into reliable, scheduled, auditable trial-ops automations — the 'always-on coworker' layer.

usecases.name: Connector/tool layer via MCP (eTMF, Smartsheet, EDC, CTMS, SharePoint, Outlook, Pinnacle 21)
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on) — invoked per agent action
usecases.what_ai_does: Exposes each system as an MCP tool with explicit, least-privilege scopes (read-mostly; writes gated). Agents read/draft through these tools; any write to a record-of-truth (eTMF filing, Smartsheet plan, EDC) is proposed and queued, not executed. The connector layer brokers and logs access — it does not loosen tier rules.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (tools called by agents on either backend, gateway-mediated)
usecases.integration: MCP servers for Veeva eTMF API, Smartsheet API, EDC, CTMS, SharePoint, Outlook/Graph, Pinnacle 21; scoped service accounts
usecases.tier: AMBER/RED (read = AMBER; write to record-of-truth = RED -> validated tool + human)
usecases.human: System owners grant scopes; record-of-truth writes require approval-queue sign-off and e-signature
usecases.value: One uniform, least-privilege, fully-logged way to reach every operational system — no brittle per-system glue, and writes can't bypass governance.

usecases.name: Unified Part 11 audit & observability trail
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on)
usecases.what_ai_does: Captures, for every gateway call and connector action: who/which-agent, model version + backend, prompt + classification decision, redactions applied, cost/tokens, tool calls, the human approval/e-signature, and before/after of any record change — into ONE tamper-evident, time-stamped store. It records; it never edits or deletes trail entries.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (collector is infra; ingests from both backends + connectors)
usecases.integration: Central log/trace store; e-signature service; exports for inspectors; immutable/WORM retention
usecases.tier: AMBER (compliance infrastructure; supports RED actions but takes no clinical decision)
usecases.human: QA/CSV owner validates the trail; inspectors read it; trail itself is access-controlled and tamper-evident
usecases.value: Makes the whole AI layer inspectable and 21 CFR Part 11 / ALCOA++-defensible — the difference between a demo and something you can put in front of an FDA inspector.

usecases.name: Human-in-the-loop approval queue
usecases.trigger: event (any agent proposes a RED/record-of-truth or timeline change)
usecases.what_ai_does: Holds every proposed change to a regulated record/timeline/reported output as a reviewable item: shows the AI draft, its source/citations, the diff, and the classification, and requires an explicit human accept/edit/reject + e-signature before the connector executes. The queue presents; the human decides and signs.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (UI is infra; proposals come from agents)
usecases.integration: Sits between orchestrator and connector writes; eTMF/Smartsheet/EDC/CTMS; e-signature; feeds audit trail
usecases.tier: RED gatekeeper (this is the mechanism that keeps the LLM out of the decision path for regulated changes)
usecases.human: Named reviewer with authority for that record/study approves, edits, or rejects with reason; logged
usecases.value: Guarantees no LLM autonomously alters a record, timeline, or reported number — humans stay accountable, with a clean approval audit.

usecases.name: Model-freeze & engine-of-record registry
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on) — consulted per run; updated on change-control
usecases.what_ai_does: Authoritative registry of (a) which frozen LOCAL model version + decoding params + prompt set is in production for each task (for reproducibility), and (b) which VALIDATED tool is the engine of record for each regulated output (Pinnacle 21 = conformance, Phoenix WinNonlin = PK, EDC/CTMS = operational truth). It pins and records; it does not let an LLM become an engine of record.
usecases.engine: Validated tool (registry governs validated engines + frozen models)
usecases.integration: References vLLM model artifacts, Pinnacle 21, Phoenix WinNonlin, EDC/CTMS; tied to change-control + audit
usecases.tier: RED-governing (defines what may produce reported numbers; LLM explicitly excluded)
usecases.human: CSV/validation + biostat leads approve every registry change under formal change-control
usecases.value: Reproducibility for inspection + a hard line that reported numbers come only from validated tools — closes the two biggest regulatory gaps of an AI stack.

usecases.name: Caching, cost/budget controls & FinOps metering
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on)
usecases.what_ai_does: Caches identical/near-identical de-identified prompts (e.g., repeated SOP retrievals) and meters tokens/cost per agent, model, study, and team via gateway virtual keys, enforcing budget caps and surfacing spend. It optimizes cost; it never trades off governance (cache is classification-aware, sensitive payloads excluded).
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (gateway feature)
usecases.integration: Gateway cache + budget engine; cost dashboards; per-study/per-team virtual keys; audit trail
usecases.human: Finance/ops owner sets budgets and alerts; reviews per-study spend
usecases.value: Predictable, attributable AI spend with cache savings on repetitive de-identified work — makes the ROI case auditable and stops runaway cloud bills.

usecases.name: Identity, RBAC & secrets / trust-boundary management
usecases.trigger: n/a (always-on)
usecases.what_ai_does: Maps users and agents to roles/studies, enforces least-privilege on gateway keys and connector scopes, holds credentials in a vault, and isolates the air-gapped LOCAL host. It authenticates/authorizes and isolates; it makes no clinical or routing-content decision beyond enforcing who-can-do-what.
usecases.engine: Local+Cloud (security infrastructure)
usecases.integration: SSO/IdP (study + role claims), secrets vault, network isolation for the GPU host, connector service accounts, audit trail
usecases.tier: AMBER (security control plane; underpins RED safeguards)
usecases.human: Security/IT owner provisions roles, scopes, and access reviews; periodic recertification
usecases.value: Ensures RAG permissions, connector writes, and unblinded-data access all respect who the user is — prevents cross-study/role leakage and keeps the air gap real.

usecases.name: Informed-consent (ICF) version tracking & site-version reconciliation
usecases.trigger: New ICF version approved; new subject consented; site IRB/EC approval received
usecases.what_ai_does: Tracks every ICF version (master + site/IRB-EC-approved local versions + translations), maps which version each enrolled subject signed against the version effective on their consent date, and flags re-consent obligations when a new version is approved mid-study. Surfaces mismatches (subject consented on a superseded version) and missing site-level approvals. Under E6(R3) consent is an ongoing dialogue/eConsent, so it tracks re-consent events too.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (ICF content + subject-level consent dates can be PHI-adjacent/unblinded; stays air-gapped). De-identified version-control logic can burst to CLOUD.
usecases.integration: eTMF, CTMS, IRB/EC tracker, EDC (consent date)
usecases.human: PM/CRA approves any re-consent trigger or flagged mismatch before action
usecases.value: Consent-version mismatches and missed re-consents are a top-5 GCP inspection finding; manual tracking across sites/languages is error-prone.

usecases.name: Site feasibility, selection & activation-readiness tracking
usecases.trigger: Feasibility responses received; activation checklist item changes; weekly status run
usecases.what_ai_does: Maintains the site activation checklist (regulatory packet, contract, IRB/EC approval, site-init visit, green-light criteria), computes per-site activation status and cycle time, drafts feasibility questionnaires and summarizes returned responses into a site-ranking matrix, and flags sites stalled on a missing prerequisite. Predicts activation-date slips from outstanding items.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified feasibility text synthesis/ranking; LOCAL if site/PI documents contain sensitive contractual/personal data.
usecases.integration: CTMS, eTMF, Smartsheet, IRB/EC tracker
usecases.human: Clin-ops lead approves go/no-go activation decisions
usecases.value: Slow/uneven site activation is the #1 cause of early-phase timeline slip; readiness gaps are easily missed across many sites.

usecases.name: Investigational-product (IP)/drug accountability & reconciliation
usecases.trigger: IP shipment/dispensing/return event; pre-monitoring-visit run; reconciliation cycle
usecases.what_ai_does: Reconciles IP shipped vs dispensed vs returned vs destroyed per site against drug-accountability logs and IXRS/IRT dispensing records; flags discrepancies, negative inventory, expiry-window breaches, temperature-excursion-quarantined lots, and missing destruction records. Drafts the accountability-discrepancy memo for CRA follow-up.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (IP/dispensing records tie to subject-level unblinded randomization data; air-gapped).
usecases.integration: IXRS/IRT, CTMS, EDC, eTMF
usecases.human: CRA/QA reconciles and signs; the reconciled count of record always comes from the validated IXRS/inventory system, not the LLM
usecases.value: Drug accountability is a mandatory GCP/Part-11 record and frequent inspection finding; reconciliation across IXRS + paper logs is tedious and error-prone.

usecases.name: Randomization / IXRS(IRT) setup-spec & UAT oversight (blinding-safe)
usecases.trigger: IXRS build/change request; UAT cycle; periodic blinded-anomaly scan
usecases.what_ai_does: Drafts and QCs the IXRS/IRT user-requirement and randomization spec against the protocol (stratification factors, block size handling, dispensing logic, emergency unblinding workflow), generates and tracks UAT test scripts and defect logs, and monitors live IXRS for stratification imbalance or assignment anomalies WITHOUT exposing treatment codes. Never touches the actual randomization list.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (randomization/IXRS internals are unblinding-sensitive; strictly air-gapped). De-identified spec drafting may use CLOUD.
usecases.integration: IXRS/IRT, EDC, CTMS
usecases.human: Unblinded statistician/QA validates randomization; LLM stays out of the unblinding decision path entirely
usecases.value: IXRS misconfiguration (wrong block/strata) can compromise an entire study; UAT oversight is detail-heavy and easily under-resourced.

usecases.name: Lab manual, central-lab spec & kit/supply management
usecases.trigger: Protocol/lab-manual update; kit-expiry threshold; inventory drawdown
usecases.what_ai_does: Maintains the lab manual and kit catalog, cross-checks the protocol's schedule-of-assessments against lab requisition panels and kit contents for coverage gaps, monitors per-site kit inventory and expiry to forecast resupply needs, and flags expiring kits or panels missing from the lab spec. Drafts resupply requests.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified protocol-vs-lab-manual coverage reasoning; LOCAL for site inventory tied to subject data.
usecases.integration: Central-lab portal, CTMS, EDC, eTMF
usecases.human: Lab-ops/CRA approves resupply orders and spec changes
usecases.value: Missing/expired kits cause un-drawable samples and protocol deviations at the visit; coverage gaps between protocol and lab spec are a classic easily-missed defect.

usecases.name: Regulatory submission & lifecycle tracking (IND/CTA, amendments, annual reports)
usecases.trigger: Submission milestone; anniversary date approaching; amendment initiated
usecases.what_ai_does: Tracks IND/CTA and amendment submission status, agency clock dates (e.g., 30-day IND safety-to-proceed, CTA assessment timelines), and recurring obligations: DSUR due on the CTA/IND anniversary, IND annual report, protocol-amendment and safety-report submissions. Maintains a submission calendar, drafts cover letters/forms (e.g., 1571-class), assembles the submission-component checklist, and alerts before deadlines.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified submission-document drafting/checklists; LOCAL if drafts contain unredacted subject or proprietary CMC data.
usecases.integration: Regulatory tracker, eTMF, CTMS, calendar
usecases.human: Regulatory affairs approves and signs all submissions; the official submission record is the regulatory system of record
usecases.value: Missed annual-report/DSUR anniversary dates and amendment-submission lapses are serious compliance failures; the recurring-clock tracking is easily forgotten.

usecases.name: Expedited safety-reporting clock tracker (SUSAR/SAE 7- & 15-day)
usecases.trigger: Day-0 awareness of a reportable case; T-minus reminders; follow-up info received
usecases.what_ai_does: Starts and tracks the regulatory clock from sponsor awareness for each reportable case: 7 calendar days for fatal/life-threatening SUSARs and 15 calendar days for other serious unexpected related events, plus follow-up-report clocks. Distributes pre-deadline reminders, tracks distribution to IRBs/ECs/investigators/agencies, and flags any case at risk of missing its clock. Drafts the distribution log, not the medical assessment.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (case safety data is PHI/unblinded; strictly air-gapped).
usecases.integration: Safety database (PV), eTMF, IRB/EC tracker, regulatory tracker
usecases.human: Pharmacovigilance/medical monitor owns causality/expectedness and the official submission; LLM only tracks/reminds
usecases.value: Missing a 7/15-day expedited-reporting clock is a severe regulatory finding; clock management across many cases and recipients is precisely the kind of bookkeeping that slips.

usecases.name: Audit & inspection logistics / readiness orchestration
usecases.trigger: Audit/inspection announced; document request issued; finding logged
usecases.what_ai_does: Manages sponsor-audit and regulatory-inspection logistics: assembles the document request list, builds and tracks the storyboard/agenda, coordinates SME availability, stands up and maintains the inspection-readiness war-room tracker, logs requests during the inspection, and tracks resulting findings to CAPA closure. Drafts responses to audit observations.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified logistics/response drafting; LOCAL for any source documents containing PHI.
usecases.integration: eTMF, CTMS, SharePoint, calendar
usecases.human: QA lead approves all inspection responses and document releases
usecases.value: Inspection logistics are high-stakes, time-compressed, and currently coordinated by hand; complements the existing inspection-readiness gap analysis with live logistics.

usecases.name: Training-compliance & qualification tracking (curriculum + delegation)
usecases.trigger: New training/protocol version assigned; delegation-log change; expiry threshold; periodic run
usecases.what_ai_does: Maintains per-person required-training matrices by role/study, reconciles LMS completion records against assignments, ties training currency to the delegation-of-authority log (no task delegated without current GCP/protocol training), and alerts on overdue training, expired GCP certs, or delegation entries lacking prerequisite training. Drafts the training-status summary for the eTMF.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified compliance reconciliation logic; LOCAL if records carry personal/HR data.
usecases.integration: LMS, eTMF (delegation log), CTMS
usecases.human: Clin-ops/PI approve delegation and training remediation
usecases.value: Untrained staff performing delegated tasks is a frequent inspection finding; the training-to-delegation linkage is easily overlooked vs simple CV-expiry alerts already on the list.

usecases.name: Data-transfer agreement (DTA) scheduling & receipt/reconciliation confirmation
usecases.trigger: Scheduled transfer due/received; spec mismatch detected
usecases.what_ai_does: Maintains the transfer calendar from each DTA/transfer spec (vendor, frequency, format, variables), confirms each expected external transfer (central lab, PK bioanalytical, ECG, IXRS, PRO/eCOA) actually arrived on schedule, validates file structure/record counts/checksums against the spec, reconciles counts against EDC, and flags missing/late/malformed transfers. Drafts the transfer-receipt log.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (transferred clinical/PK data is PHI/unblinded-adjacent; air-gapped). Spec-conformance metadata can use CLOUD.
usecases.integration: SFTP/transfer system, EDC, DTA/transfer spec, CTMS
usecases.human: DM lead confirms acceptance; reported counts come from validated reconciliation, not the LLM
usecases.value: Silent missed or malformed external data transfers surface late and jeopardize DB-lock timelines; receipt confirmation against the DTA schedule is routinely manual.

usecases.name: Contract / CDA / agreement lifecycle tracking (CTA-contract, CDA, MSA, vendor SOWs)
usecases.trigger: Agreement drafted/executed; expiry/renewal window; new vendor or site onboarding
usecases.what_ai_does: Tracks the full agreement portfolio (confidentiality/CDAs, clinical-trial-agreement contracts with sites, MSAs, vendor SOWs, change orders) with execution status, effective/expiry dates, auto-renewal and notice windows, and key obligation extraction (deliverables, payment triggers). Alerts on unexecuted agreements blocking site activation and on expiring CDAs before data sharing.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified clause extraction/summary; LOCAL for fully-executed contracts with sensitive commercial terms.
usecases.integration: Contract repository, CTMS, eTMF, SharePoint
usecases.human: Legal/contracts approves; LLM extracts and reminds only
usecases.value: Unexecuted CTAs/CDAs silently block activation and create data-sharing exposure; obligation/expiry tracking is fragmented across legal and ops.

usecases.name: Lessons-learned & continuous-improvement knowledge capture
usecases.trigger: Study close-out; CAPA closure; new study kickoff; periodic mining run
usecases.what_ai_does: Mines closed studies, CAPAs, deviation trends, query patterns, and meeting minutes to draft a structured lessons-learned register, clusters recurring issues into themes, and at study start-up surfaces relevant prior lessons (via the RAG KB) for the new protocol. Feeds candidate process improvements back to SOP owners.
usecases.engine: CLOUD for de-identified thematic synthesis; LOCAL for source artifacts containing study-sensitive content.
usecases.integration: RAG KB, eTMF, CTMS, CAPA tracker
usecases.human: QA/process owner curates the register before it becomes guidance
usecases.value: Lessons-learned is universally intended and almost never systematically done; turns dead artifacts into reusable institutional knowledge feeding E6(R3) continuous quality improvement.

usecases.name: Sponsor-update / newsletter & external communication drafting
usecases.trigger: Scheduled cadence (weekly/monthly); major milestone reached
usecases.what_ai_does: Auto-drafts recurring sponsor updates and study newsletters by assembling enrollment metrics, milestone status, safety summary headlines, and upcoming events from CTMS/dashboards into the house template; tailors versions for sponsor vs sites; and routes for review. Maintains a distribution log.
usecases.engine: CLOUD on de-identified aggregate metrics/narrative; LOCAL if any subject-level or unblinded figures are referenced.
usecases.integration: CTMS, dashboards, Outlook, SharePoint
usecases.human: PM reviews and sends; no unblinded data in any externally distributed draft
usecases.value: Sponsor communications are time-consuming, recurring, and template-driven; a clear win that is easily missed in an ops automation catalog.

usecases.name: Subject travel, reimbursement & visit-logistics coordination (early-phase unit ops)
usecases.trigger: Cohort scheduled; visit-window approaching; capacity conflict detected
usecases.what_ai_does: For early-phase/Phase-1-unit studies, schedules confinement and ambulatory PK visits against the protocol's strict time windows, tracks bed/slot capacity for confinement cohorts, manages subject travel/stipend/reimbursement workflows, and flags visit-window risks or capacity conflicts across overlapping cohorts. Drafts subject visit reminders.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (subject identity, travel, payment data is PHI; air-gapped).
usecases.integration: CTMS, clinic scheduling system, payment/stipend system, EDC
usecases.human: Unit coordinator approves schedules and reimbursements
usecases.value: Phase-1 confinement scheduling and rigid PK-window logistics are operationally intense and unit-specific; entirely absent from the current catalog and core to an early-phase clin-pharm CRO.

usecases.name: eCOA / ePRO / wearable-device data oversight & compliance monitoring
usecases.trigger: ePRO/device data refresh; compliance threshold breach; scheduled run
usecases.what_ai_does: Monitors eCOA/ePRO and connected-device/wearable data streams for completeness and compliance (missed diary entries, low device-wear time, implausible/curbstoned entry patterns, device sync gaps), reconciles eCOA records against expected visit/diary schedule, and drafts site-level compliance alerts. Flags data-integrity patterns for central monitoring.
usecases.engine: LOCAL (ePRO/device data is subject-level PHI; air-gapped). De-identified pattern stats may use CLOUD.
usecases.integration: eCOA/ePRO platform, device data hub, EDC, RBQM dashboard
usecases.human: DM/central-monitoring reviews flags; LLM does not adjudicate compliance status
usecases.value: eCOA/device compliance and entry-integrity gaps are easily missed and directly threaten endpoint validity; distinct from the EDC-centric edit-check and reconciliation items already listed.

facts: Smartsheet exposes a REST API with webhooks (now supporting event filtering), cell/row update endpoints, and dependency-enabled sheets that compute critical path from start/end dates, duration, and predecessor links — so a gateway agent can listen for a study event, recompute the plan, write cells, and post notifications programmatically (https://developers.smartsheet.com/api/smartsheet/openapi/webhooks).

Critical path in Smartsheet is the longest dependency chain that drives the finish date; a milestone slip on any critical-path task cascades downstream, which is exactly the recompute an automation must surface (https://help.smartsheet.com/learning-track/level-3-solutions/critical-summary-and-driving-paths).

ICH E6(R3) makes RBQM pervasive: trial-level Quality Tolerance Limits / 'acceptable ranges' and site-level Key Risk Indicators feed risk-register and timeline-risk decisions, tying PM directly to quality oversight (https://cluepoints.com/decoding-ich-e6r3-what-it-means-for-risk-based-quality-management-rbqm/).

Governance routing: PHI/unblinded/IP-sensitive content stays on the LOCAL frozen open-weight model (zero egress, air-gapped); de-identified status text, plans, and reasoning go to CLOUD Claude under BAA — both behind one self-hosted LiteLLM-class OpenAI-compatible gateway.

Reported/regulated timeline records and budget actuals live in systems of record (CTMS, Smartsheet plan-of-record, finance/ERP); the LLM drafts/classifies/triages/summarizes and never autonomously commits a change to a regulated record — humans approve, with a 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and ALCOA++.

Tiering applied here: GREEN = drafts, summaries, RAG status answers; AMBER = classification/triage/critical-path recompute proposals with human verify before the Smartsheet write; RED = anything that finalizes a regulated milestone date, contractual deliverable date, or budget actual of record.

MCP-style connectors let the orchestration layer reach Smartsheet, Outlook/Exchange, SharePoint, the CTMS/EDC, and the RAG store as tools, while a scheduler (cron/n8n/Power Automate) fires the time-based agents (data cut, weekly status, monthly portfolio rollup).

A study-document RAG knowledge base (protocol, monitoring plan, contracts, SOWs, charters) grounds status, risk, and vendor-oversight drafting so the agent cites the plan-of-record rather than hallucinating dates or obligations.

Early-phase clin-pharm programs run many short, parallel cohorts/studies, so cross-study portfolio rollups and shared-resource (PK lab, clinic beds, programmer) capacity conflicts are first-order PM risks the catalog must address.
Smartsheet update requests / proofs and an explicit human-in-the-loop approval queue provide the Part 11-defensible checkpoint between an AI-proposed plan change and the committed record.

The DIA TMF Reference Model v3.2.0 (current, MIT-licensed via CDISC stewardship) defines a standardized three-level taxonomy — Zones (top-level, e.g. 01 Trial Oversight, 02 Central Trial Documents, 04 IMP, 05 Site Management), Sections, and ~600+ customizable sub-artifacts, each with a unique artifact ID — and is the de-facto target structure into which any auto-classifier must map a document.
