ICH E6(R3) GCP (effective 2025) reinforces that the TMF must be contemporaneous, complete, and inspection-ready at all times, with ALCOA++ attributes; this makes continuous completeness/QC and gap detection an ongoing obligation, not a pre-inspection scramble.

21 CFR Part 11 governs the eTMF as an electronic record system: filing/superseding/declaring record-of-truth requires an audit trail, e-signatures, and human-approved actions — so the LLM may classify and propose, but a validated eTMF (e.g. Veeva Vault eTMF, Montrium, Phlexglobal) and a human own the final filing decision (RED/AMBER).

Routing rule applied throughout: study documents frequently embed PHI/PII (subject IDs on source-linked docs, signed ICFs, monitoring notes, CVs with personal data) — these go to the LOCAL frozen model with zero egress; only confirmed de-identified text is eligible for the CLOUD Claude API under BAA.

Microsoft Presidio (open-source, GPU-accelerated, now with a MedicalNERRecognizer and DICOM image redaction) is the de-identification/PII-PHI guardrail that runs at the gateway before any cloud call and flags documents that must stay local.

A permissioned local vector store (RAG) over SOPs, WIs, and study-specific documents is the substrate for compliance Q&A; chunking + embeddings live on-prem, answers cite the source SOP/section, and access mirrors the document-management system's permissions.

Validated eTMF / DMS platforms expose APIs and metadata (artifact ID, zone, section, version, status, expiry, signature state) that connectors/MCP can read for completeness analytics and write-back of proposed metadata to a staging/approval queue — never directly to the record-of-truth.

Inspection-readiness is measured against an expected-document index (the TMF plan / artifact-to-milestone map): completeness is the delta between expected artifacts for the current study phase/site and what is actually filed, scored per zone.

Credential currency (investigator/sub-I CVs, GCP training certs, medical licenses, lab certifications/normal ranges, IATA shipping certs) carries explicit expiry dates extractable from documents; an alerting agent watches these and warns before lapse, a top source of inspection findings.

Version/superseded control is a structural requirement: when a new SOP/protocol amendment/ICF version is filed, prior versions must be marked superseded and downstream references reconciled — the LLM detects the supersession event and drafts the reconciliation, a human confirms the status change in the validated system.

Validated tools own pass/fail; the LLM never does. Pinnacle 21 Enterprise (Certara) runs the CDISC conformance ruleset (FDA, PMDA Validation Rules 3.0, and CDISC CORE engine) against SDTMIG v3.4 / ADaMIG v1.3 / Define-XML v2.1 and emits the authoritative findings file (Error/Warning/Notice). The hybrid LLM only triages, explains, clusters, drafts the disposition, and routes that file — it is out of the pass/fail decision path (RED for the report-of-record itself).

Data-sensitivity routing is absolute: subject-level / unblinded / PHI rows (raw EDC extracts, lab values, PK concentrations, AE narratives, listings) are processed ONLY by the LOCAL frozen open-weight model on the air-gapped GPU box (zero egress). De-identified, aggregate, or metadata-only text (a finding code, a rule description, a count, a SAP rule paraphrase) may go to CLOUD Claude for harder reasoning. Sensitivity is decided before reasoning difficulty.

ICH E6(R3) RBQM is the governing frame for recurrent QC: Quality Tolerance Limits (QTLs) operate at TRIAL level (now framed as 'acceptable ranges'), Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) at SITE level. E6(R3) expects sponsors to demonstrate centralized statistical monitoring (CSM), not just describe it — which is exactly what scheduled cross-form, outlier, and trend QC delivers.

ALCOA++ and 21 CFR Part 11: every automated check run, every AI triage suggestion, and every human disposition is captured in one unified Part 11 audit trail (who/what/when/why, attributable, contemporaneous, original, accurate, plus complete/consistent/enduring/available). The LLM's output is an audited suggestion, never an applied change to a regulated record.

Reported/regulated numbers are never produced or 'corrected' by an LLM: PK parameters come from Phoenix WinNonlin (engine of record), CDISC conformance from Pinnacle 21, the data values of record from the EDC/CTMS. The AI can flag a suspected discrepancy but the authoritative number always re-derives from the validated tool.

The RAG knowledge base (local, permissioned vector store over SOPs/WIs, the Data Review Plan, the SAP, BLQ/LLOQ rules, edit-check specs, the cSDRG/ADRG) grounds every triage explanation — so a finding is explained against the study's own rules and the prior-finding disposition history, not generic model priors.

Orchestration is scheduled-job + agent-framework (cron/Python or n8n/Power Automate) firing through the LiteLLM-class gateway; connectors (Pinnacle 21 output share, EDC/CTMS API, IXRS/IRT, the bioanalytical lab transfer, the query manager, SharePoint, Outlook/Teams) are reached as tools, possibly via MCP. Presidio-class PII/PHI guardrails sit at the gateway as a backstop to the routing rule.

Recurrence is the point: these run at EVERY stage on EVERY data refresh/transfer (not once at lock) — incremental DTA loads, blinded data reviews, dry-runs, and the pre-lock window — so quality is continuous (E6(R3) 'quality by design') rather than an end-of-study scramble. Each run is delta-aware: AI summarizes what changed since the last clean run.

ICH E6(R3) (Step 4, finalized Jan 2025; effective ~July 2025) makes Risk-Based Quality Management (RBQM) the operating model: identify Critical-to-Quality (CtQ) factors, then monitor risks via Key Risk Indicators (KRIs, site-level) and Quality Tolerance Limits (QTLs, trial-level). QTL breaches are deviations potentially impacting subject safety or result reliability and must be documented in the CSR.

KRIs compare site-level performance against other sites or benchmarks (e.g., query rate, SAE/AE ratio, deviation rate, missing-data rate, screen-fail rate); QTLs are pre-specified trial-level thresholds on a small set of critical parameters. An LLM can draft/maintain KRI/QTL definitions and triage breach signals, but the breach math/flagging should run in the validated CSM/RBQM platform.

Central Statistical Monitoring (CSM) detects site outliers, digit preference (e.g., volcano plot: -log10 p-value vs max digit-frequency difference site-vs-rest), values too close/far from means, anomalous variances/correlations, improbable dates, and under-reporting of AEs — statistical methods that are reproducibility-sensitive and belong in a frozen/validated engine, with the LLM only narrating and triaging the outputs.

DIA TMF Reference Model is the eTMF zone/section taxonomy; monitoring artifacts (MVRs, monitoring plans, communication logs, deviation logs) map to TMF Zone 5 (Trial Management) / Zone 4-5; the eTMF (Veeva Vault) is the record-of-truth and any record change is RED (validated system + human).

Reported/regulated safety numbers (AE/SAE counts, lab shift tables, exposure) ALWAYS come from validated tools (EDC/safety DB, SAS/Phoenix outputs) with medical review — never an LLM. Pinnacle 21 governs CDISC conformance; Phoenix WinNonlin governs PK; the EDC/CTMS is the system of record.

Governance routing: unblinded/subject-level/PHI signals -> LOCAL frozen open-weight model (zero egress, air-gapped GPU workstation); de-identified aggregate text and hard reasoning -> CLOUD Claude API under BAA; a self-hosted LiteLLM-class OpenAI-compatible gateway enforces the route by data-sensitivity classification.

MCP (Model Context Protocol) connectors let agents pull/push to Smartsheet (CAPA/action-tracking, via Smartsheet API), SharePoint/Outlook (distribution, MVR follow-up), the RAG store (SOPs/WIs/monitoring plan/protocol), and the CSM platform/EDC exports — read-mostly, with writes gated to non-regulated trackers and human-approved.

ALCOA++ and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail apply to every step: each LLM draft, classification, route decision, and human approval is logged (who/what/when/why, model+version, prompt/version) so monitoring outputs are attributable, contemporaneous, and reconstructable.

Tiering: GREEN = drafts/summaries/RAG Q&A (MVR summaries, packet narrative drafts); AMBER = classification/triage/code with human verify (deviation classification, signal triage, KRI definition code); RED = anything touching reported numbers, eTMF record-of-truth, QTL-breach determinations of record, or unblinded data -> validated tool + human, LLM out of the decision path.

LiteLLM is, as of 2026, the de-facto self-hosted OpenAI-compatible LLM gateway/proxy: one OpenAI-shaped API in front of 100+ backends, with built-in fallbacks, per-key/per-model budgets, virtual keys, request logging and caching — so every agent calls one endpoint and routing/cost/audit are enforced centrally, not in each script.

Microsoft Presidio is the PII/PHI detection-and-redaction engine wired into the gateway as a pre-call guardrail (LiteLLM ships a Presidio guardrail): it detects and masks identifiers before any text can leave the trust boundary, supporting HIPAA/GDPR de-identification. It is the enforcement point for the PHI/unblinded -> LOCAL routing rule.

Routing is by DATA CLASSIFICATION, not by task: PHI / unblinded / subject-level text is pinned to the LOCAL backend (zero egress, air-gapped); only de-identified text is eligible for the CLOUD Claude backend. The gateway, not the human, makes egress impossible for misclassified-sensitive payloads via Presidio + a deny-by-default policy.

The LOCAL backend is an open-weight model served by vLLM on a GPU workstation (OpenAI-compatible server), giving high-throughput on-prem inference; the CLOUD backend is the Claude API under a signed BAA. Claude Max/consumer plans are NOT HIPAA-eligible — only the API-with-BAA path is used for any regulated context.

MCP (Model Context Protocol), open-sourced by Anthropic Nov 2024 and donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation in Dec 2025, is the de-facto standard for connecting agents to tools/data; it gives the platform a uniform, auditable connector layer (eTMF, Smartsheet, EDC, CTMS, SharePoint, Outlook, Pinnacle 21) instead of bespoke per-system glue.

Reported/regulated numbers ALWAYS come from a validated engine of record — Pinnacle 21 for CDISC (SDTM/ADaM) conformance, Phoenix WinNonlin for PK, the validated EDC/CTMS — never from an LLM. The engine-of-record registry names which validated tool owns each regulated output so an LLM number can never be mistaken for the source of truth.

21 CFR Part 11 + ALCOA++ require a secure, attributable, time-stamped, tamper-evident audit trail with e-signatures and human approval of changes to regulated records. The platform routes every gateway call, every connector write, and every human approval into ONE unified Part 11 trail so the AI layer is inspectable end-to-end.

ICH E6(R3) GCP (final Jan 2025; EU effective Jul 2025; US compliance date pending as of 2026) mandates risk-proportionate oversight (centralized/statistical monitoring, KRIs/QTLs), which is what the orchestration + monitoring agents operationalize — but signal -> human decision stays human, AI only triages.

Model-freeze: the LOCAL open-weight model is version-pinned (frozen weights + fixed decoding params + fixed prompts) so a given analysis-support run is reproducible and re-runnable years later for inspection — a property a moving cloud model cannot guarantee, which is why frozen-local handles anything that must be reproduced.
